tasks.title: Generate player head sprite asset (PixelLab)
tasks.description: Use PixelLab MCP to generate a 32x32 top-down player head sprite that reads clearly when rotated in all four cardinal directions
tasks.details: Call mcp__pixellab__create_object with description='player creature head, top-down view, clear directionality', size=32, directions=1, view='high top-down'. The sprite must be designed so it can be rotated 0°/90°/180°/270° in code to represent facing right/down/left/up. Save the resulting sprite to snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/player_head.png. Ensure the sprite has a consistent pixel-art style that will match other game assets.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify the sprite file exists at snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/player_head.png, is 32x32 pixels, and visually reads clearly when rotated. Import into Godot and test rotation at 0°, 90°, 180°, 270° to confirm directional clarity.

tasks.title: Generate player body segment sprite asset (PixelLab)
tasks.description: Use PixelLab MCP to generate a 32x32 top-down body segment sprite that tiles cleanly when placed in adjacent grid cells
tasks.details: Call mcp__pixellab__create_object with description='player creature body segment, top-down view, tileable', size=32, directions=1, view='high top-down'. The sprite should visually connect when placed next to itself in a chain. Save to snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/player_body.png. Match the art style of the player head sprite.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify sprite exists at snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/player_body.png, is 32x32 pixels. Place multiple copies adjacent in Godot to confirm clean tiling with no visual gaps or misalignment.

tasks.title: Generate food item sprite asset (PixelLab)
tasks.description: Use PixelLab MCP to generate a 32x32 top-down food item sprite (e.g., apple or similar collectible)
tasks.details: Call mcp__pixellab__create_object with description='food item for game, apple or fruit, top-down view, appetizing', size=32, directions=1, view='high top-down'. Save to snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/food.png. Ensure style consistency with player sprites.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify sprite exists at snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/food.png, is 32x32 pixels, and is visually distinct and appealing as a collectible item.

tasks.title: Generate background floor tile asset (PixelLab)
tasks.description: Use PixelLab MCP to generate a 32x32 seamlessly tileable background floor sprite for the playfield
tasks.details: Call mcp__pixellab__create_tiles_pro with description='1). grass floor tile, seamless, simple pattern', tile_size=32, tile_type='square_topdown', tile_view='top-down'. Save to snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/floor_tile.png. The tile must tile perfectly across a 20x15 grid with no visible seams.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify sprite exists at snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/floor_tile.png, is 32x32 pixels. Create a TileMap in Godot and fill a 20x15 area to confirm seamless tiling with no visible edges or pattern breaks.

tasks.title: Generate wall border tile asset (PixelLab)
tasks.description: Use PixelLab MCP to generate a 32x32 wall/border sprite that tiles cleanly when forming a continuous border
tasks.details: Call mcp__pixellab__create_tiles_pro with description='1). wall tile, border, solid barrier, stone or brick', tile_size=32, tile_type='square_topdown', tile_view='low top-down'. Save to snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/wall_tile.png. Must tile cleanly when placed in a continuous rectangular border.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify sprite exists at snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/wall_tile.png, is 32x32 pixels. Place tiles in a rectangular border pattern in Godot to confirm clean tiling along edges and corners.

tasks.title: Set up project directory structure and asset organization
tasks.description: Create the necessary folder structure for the Godot project including assets, scripts, scenes, and audio directories
tasks.details: Create the following directory structure under snaketaskmaster/: assets/sprites/, assets/audio/, scenes/, scripts/. Ensure all folders are properly recognized by Godot. This provides a clean organizational structure for all game assets and code files.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify all directories exist: snaketaskmaster/assets/sprites/, snaketaskmaster/assets/audio/, snaketaskmaster/scenes/, snaketaskmaster/scripts/. Open the Godot editor and confirm folders are visible in the FileSystem dock.

tasks.title: Implement grid-based coordinate system and game constants
tasks.description: Create a GameConstants GDScript autoload singleton defining grid dimensions (20x15), cell size (32px), tick interval (0.15s), and coordinate conversion utilities
tasks.details: Create scripts/game_constants.gd as an autoload singleton. Define constants: GRID_WIDTH=20, GRID_HEIGHT=15, CELL_SIZE=32, TICK_INTERVAL=0.15. Add helper functions: grid_to_pixel(grid_pos: Vector2i) -> Vector2, pixel_to_grid(pixel_pos: Vector2) -> Vector2i, is_valid_grid_pos(pos: Vector2i) -> bool (checks 0 <= x < 20, 0 <= y < 15). Register the autoload in project settings as 'GameConstants'.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify scripts/game_constants.gd exists and is registered as autoload. Test coordinate conversion functions with known values (e.g., grid(0,0) -> pixel(16,16) for centered, grid(19,14) -> pixel valid). Confirm is_valid_grid_pos correctly rejects (-1,0) and (20,0) and accepts (0,0) and (19,14).

tasks.title: Implement player entity (head and body chain) logic
tasks.description: Create Player node with head and body segment management, including position tracking, growth mechanics, and visual representation using generated sprites
tasks.details: Create scripts/player.gd extending Node2D. Properties: segments (Array[Vector2i]) starting with [Vector2i(10,7), Vector2i(9,7), Vector2i(8,7)], current_direction (Vector2i.RIGHT), next_direction (Vector2i.RIGHT), grow_pending (int = 0). Methods: _ready() to initialize sprites for head and body; move(direction: Vector2i) to advance head and shift body segments (or grow if grow_pending > 0); add_growth() to increment grow_pending; get_head_position() -> Vector2i; occupies_position(pos: Vector2i) -> bool. Use the player_head.png sprite for index 0 (rotated based on direction) and player_body.png for all other segments. Position sprites using GameConstants.grid_to_pixel().
tasks.testStrategy: Instantiate Player in a test scene. Verify initial 3-segment chain renders correctly at starting position (10,7). Call move() multiple times and confirm head advances while body follows. Call add_growth() then move() and verify length increases by 1. Test occupies_position() returns true for all segment positions and false for empty cells.

tasks.title: Implement input handling with arrow keys and WASD
tasks.description: Create InputManager to handle directional input from arrow keys and WASD, with 180-degree reversal prevention and input buffering between ticks
tasks.details: Create scripts/input_manager.gd as an autoload singleton. In _input(event), check for ui_up/ui_down/ui_left/ui_right (arrow keys) and map WASD keys (KEY_W/A/S/D) to the same directions. Store the most recent valid direction in buffered_direction (Vector2i). Provide method get_buffered_direction(current_direction: Vector2i) -> Vector2i that returns the buffered direction only if it's not 180° opposite to current_direction (i.e., new_dir != -current_dir), otherwise returns current_direction. Clear buffer after retrieval. Register as autoload 'InputManager'.
tasks.testStrategy: Register InputManager as autoload. In a test scene, simulate key presses and verify get_buffered_direction() returns correct directions. Test reversal blocking: if current direction is RIGHT (1,0), pressing LEFT should be ignored and RIGHT returned. Verify both arrow keys and WASD produce identical behavior.

tasks.title: Implement tick-based game loop and movement system
tasks.description: Create GameLoop manager that advances the game state at fixed 0.15s intervals, processing input and moving the player one grid cell per tick
tasks.details: Create scripts/game_loop.gd as an autoload singleton or attach to main scene root. Use a Timer node set to 0.15s wait_time, one_shot=false, autostart=false. Properties: is_active (bool). On timeout signal, call _on_tick() which: 1) gets next_direction from InputManager.get_buffered_direction(player.current_direction), 2) updates player.current_direction, 3) calls player.move(current_direction), 4) checks collision conditions (wall or self-collision). Methods: start_game() to reset and start timer, stop_game() to stop timer. Integrate with Player node reference.
tasks.testStrategy: Start the game loop and verify tick callback fires every 0.15 seconds (±0.01s). Place player in scene and confirm it moves one cell per tick in the current direction. Press arrow key between ticks and verify direction changes on next tick. Verify timer stops when stop_game() is called.

tasks.title: Implement food spawning and collision detection
tasks.description: Create FoodManager to spawn a single food item at a random empty grid cell and detect when the player head collides with it
tasks.details: Create scripts/food_manager.gd extending Node2D. Properties: current_food_position (Vector2i), food_sprite (Sprite2D). Methods: spawn_food(occupied_cells: Array[Vector2i]) selects a uniformly random grid position not in occupied_cells and not a wall, updates current_food_position, positions the food sprite using GameConstants.grid_to_pixel(). Load food.png sprite in _ready(). check_collision(head_pos: Vector2i) -> bool returns true if head_pos == current_food_position. Expose signal food_eaten.
tasks.testStrategy: Instantiate FoodManager in test scene. Call spawn_food() with player segment positions and verify food sprite appears at a valid empty cell. Move player head to food position and verify check_collision() returns true. Verify food_eaten signal emits. Call spawn_food() again and confirm food moves to a new empty position.

tasks.title: Integrate food eating with player growth and scoring
tasks.description: Connect food collision detection to player growth logic and score tracking, spawning new food after each consumption
tasks.details: In the game loop (scripts/game_loop.gd or main scene script), after player.move(), call food_manager.check_collision(player.get_head_position()). If true: 1) increment score (tracked in GameState singleton or scene variable), 2) call player.add_growth(), 3) call food_manager.spawn_food(player.segments), 4) emit food_eaten signal (for audio). Create scripts/game_state.gd autoload with properties: current_score (int), high_score (int). Provide methods: add_score(amount: int), reset_score(), update_high_score().
tasks.testStrategy: Start game, move player to food position. Verify score increments by 1, player length increases by 1 on next move, and new food spawns at a different empty cell. Eat multiple food items and confirm continuous growth and scoring. Verify high_score updates when current_score exceeds it.

tasks.title: Implement wall collision detection
tasks.description: Detect when the player's head moves outside the valid 20x15 grid boundaries and trigger game-over condition
tasks.details: In the game loop _on_tick() method (after player.move()), check if player.get_head_position() is outside the valid grid using !GameConstants.is_valid_grid_pos(head_pos). If true, call game_over() method which stops the game loop timer and transitions to game-over state. Store collision type ('wall' or 'self') in GameState for game-over screen display.
tasks.testStrategy: Position player near the right wall (x=19) facing right. After next tick, verify game-over is triggered when head reaches x=20. Repeat for all four walls (x=-1, x=20, y=-1, y=15). Confirm game loop stops and collision type is set to 'wall'.

tasks.title: Implement self-collision detection
tasks.description: Detect when the player's head collides with any body segment and trigger game-over condition
tasks.details: In the game loop _on_tick() method (after player.move() and wall check), call player.occupies_position(player.get_head_position()) excluding the head itself (check segments[1..n]). If collision detected, call game_over() with collision type 'self'. Ensure the check only evaluates body segments, not the head position against itself.
tasks.testStrategy: Create a test scenario where player is forced into a self-collision (e.g., U-turn pattern with sufficient length). Verify game-over triggers when head position matches any body segment position. Confirm collision type is set to 'self' and game loop stops.

tasks.title: Create playfield rendering with background tiles and walls
tasks.description: Build the visual playfield using TileMap nodes for the 20x15 floor grid and surrounding wall border, using generated tile sprites
tasks.details: In scenes/main.tscn (or create scenes/playfield.tscn), add a TileMapLayer node for the floor. Create a TileSet resource with floor_tile.png (32x32, tile index 0). Fill the 20x15 grid (x: 0-19, y: 0-14) with the floor tile. Add a second TileMapLayer for walls. Create a TileSet with wall_tile.png. Place wall tiles at the border: x=-1 and x=20 for all y=-1 to y=15, y=-1 and y=15 for all x=-1 to x=20. Position the TileMapLayer so grid (0,0) aligns with pixel position (0,0) or appropriate offset.
tasks.testStrategy: Open scenes/playfield.tscn (or main.tscn) in Godot editor. Verify floor tiles fill a 20x15 rectangle with no gaps. Verify wall tiles form a continuous border around all four edges. Run the scene and confirm tiles render correctly with no seams or alignment issues.

tasks.title: Create title screen scene with high score display and input prompt
tasks.description: Build the title screen UI showing game name, current high score (from memory), and 'Press any key to start' prompt
tasks.details: Create scenes/title_screen.tscn with a CanvasLayer root. Add child nodes: Label for game title '2D Grid Survival Game' (centered, large font), Label for 'High Score: [score]' (bind to GameState.high_score), Label for 'Press any key to start' (centered, smaller font, consider blinking animation using AnimationPlayer). In scripts/title_screen.gd, override _input(event) to detect any key press (event is InputEventKey and event.pressed) and call get_tree().change_scene_to_file('res://scenes/game.tscn') to transition to gameplay.
tasks.testStrategy: Load scenes/title_screen.tscn and verify all UI elements render correctly. Set GameState.high_score to a test value and confirm it displays. Press any key and verify scene transitions to game scene. Verify high score persists across multiple runs within the same session.

tasks.title: Create game-over screen with final score and restart prompt
tasks.description: Build the game-over UI showing final score, new high score indicator (if applicable), and 'Press any key to restart' prompt
tasks.details: Create scenes/game_over_screen.tscn with a CanvasLayer root. Add child nodes: Label for 'Game Over' (centered, large font), Label for 'Final Score: [score]' (bind to GameState.current_score), Label for 'New High Score!' (visible only if current_score > previous high score, use GameState to track), Label for 'Press any key to restart' (centered). In scripts/game_over_screen.gd, override _input(event) to detect any key press and call get_tree().change_scene_to_file('res://scenes/title_screen.tscn'). Accept a 'collision_type' parameter to optionally display how the player died.
tasks.testStrategy: Trigger game-over from game scene and verify transition to game_over_screen.tscn. Confirm final score displays correctly. Achieve a new high score and verify 'New High Score!' label appears. Press any key and verify return to title screen. Verify high score persists.

tasks.title: Create main game scene integrating all gameplay components
tasks.description: Build the primary gameplay scene (game.tscn) that integrates playfield, player, food manager, HUD, and game loop
tasks.details: Create scenes/game.tscn with a Node2D root. Add child nodes: Playfield (from task 27 or instantiate the TileMapLayers directly), Player (from task 20), FoodManager (from task 23), CanvasLayer for HUD with Label 'Score: [score]' (bind to GameState.current_score), GameLoop timer/script (from task 22). In scripts/game.gd, implement _ready() to initialize game state (GameState.reset_score(), player.reset(), food_manager.spawn_food(player.segments), game_loop.start_game()). Implement game_over() to transition to game_over_screen.tscn. Set this scene as the main scene or accessible from title screen.
tasks.testStrategy: Load scenes/game.tscn. Verify all components render: playfield with tiles, player at starting position, food spawned, HUD showing score 0. Start game loop and confirm player moves, can eat food, score increments, and game-over triggers on collision. Verify smooth integration of all systems.

tasks.title: Implement HUD score display with real-time updates
tasks.description: Create a HUD overlay that displays the current score during gameplay and updates in real-time as food is eaten
tasks.details: In scenes/game.tscn's CanvasLayer HUD, add a Label node positioned at top-left or top-center. In scripts/hud.gd or game.gd, connect to GameState signals or use _process() to update the label text to 'Score: ' + str(GameState.current_score). Ensure the label uses a readable font size and contrasting color against the playfield background. Consider adding a semi-transparent background panel for better readability.
tasks.testStrategy: Start game and verify HUD displays 'Score: 0'. Eat food and confirm score updates to 1, 2, 3, etc. in real-time. Verify HUD remains visible and readable throughout gameplay against all background variations.

tasks.title: Add placeholder audio SFX for food eating
tasks.description: Integrate a simple audio SFX that plays when the player eats food, using free/CC0 or generated placeholder sound
tasks.details: Find or generate a short blip/positive tone sound effect (free/CC0 license, e.g., from freesound.org or using a simple sine wave generator). Save as assets/audio/eat_food.wav or .ogg. In FoodManager or game.gd, add an AudioStreamPlayer node. Load eat_food audio file. When food_eaten signal emits (task 24), call audio_player.play(). Ensure volume is at an appropriate level (not too loud).
tasks.testStrategy: Eat food during gameplay and verify a short audio blip plays. Confirm sound is audible but not jarring. Test multiple food eats in quick succession to ensure audio doesn't clip or overlap poorly.

tasks.title: Add placeholder audio SFX for player death
tasks.description: Integrate a simple audio SFX that plays when the player dies (wall or self-collision), using free/CC0 or generated placeholder sound
tasks.details: Find or generate a short descending tone or negative sound effect (free/CC0 license). Save as assets/audio/death.wav or .ogg. In game.gd or game_loop.gd, add an AudioStreamPlayer node. Load death audio file. In the game_over() method (tasks 25, 26), call audio_player.play() before transitioning to game-over screen. Add a small delay (e.g., await get_tree().create_timer(0.5).timeout) to let the sound play before scene change.
tasks.testStrategy: Trigger game-over by colliding with wall or self. Verify descending tone plays before game-over screen appears. Confirm audio completes before scene transition (or use await to ensure proper timing).

tasks.title: Implement window settings and integer pixel scaling
tasks.description: Configure Godot project settings for proper window size, viewport scaling, and integer pixel scaling to keep pixel art crisp
tasks.details: In project.godot, configure: display/window/size/viewport_width=640 (20 cells × 32px), viewport_height=480 (15 cells × 32px), display/window/size/mode=0 (windowed), display/window/stretch/mode='viewport', display/window/stretch/aspect='keep'. For integer scaling, set rendering/2d/snap/snap_2d_transforms_to_pixel=true and rendering/2d/snap/snap_2d_vertices_to_pixel=true. Ensure the camera (if any) is set to pixel-perfect settings. Test at different window sizes to confirm pixels remain crisp without blurring.
tasks.testStrategy: Run the game and verify the window size is 640x480 (or scales proportionally). Resize window and confirm pixel art remains crisp with integer scaling (no blurring or anti-aliasing). Verify all sprites render at exact pixel boundaries without sub-pixel positioning artifacts.

tasks.title: Final integration testing and polish
tasks.description: Perform end-to-end testing of the complete game loop from title screen through gameplay to game-over, fixing any bugs or polish issues
tasks.details: Test complete game flow: 1) Start at title screen, verify high score display and key press to start. 2) Play game, move in all directions, eat food, verify growth and scoring. 3) Test wall collision on all four walls. 4) Test self-collision by making player run into its own body. 5) Verify game-over screen shows correct score and high score indicator. 6) Restart from game-over, verify return to title screen. 7) Test input buffering and 180° reversal blocking. 8) Verify audio plays correctly. 9) Fix any visual glitches, timing issues, or edge cases discovered. 10) Ensure consistent 0.15s tick rate under normal load.
tasks.testStrategy: Complete at least 5 full game runs from title to game-over. Achieve various scores (low, medium, high). Test all collision types. Verify high score persistence across runs. Confirm no crashes, visual glitches, or gameplay bugs. Verify all acceptance criteria from PRD section 8 are met.

tasks.title: Update project README with game instructions and build information
tasks.description: Document the game's controls, objective, and how to run/build the project in the README.md file
tasks.details: Update README.md at project root with sections: 1) Game Overview (objective, mechanics), 2) Controls (Arrow Keys / WASD for movement), 3) How to Run (open in Godot 4.6, press F5), 4) How to Build (export instructions for Windows desktop), 5) Credits (PixelLab MCP for assets, any audio sources), 6) License (if applicable). Keep it concise and user-friendly for anyone cloning the repo.
tasks.testStrategy: Read the README.md and verify all sections are clear and accurate. Follow the 'How to Run' instructions from a fresh clone to confirm they work. Verify controls and game objective are accurately described.
